info.name: Maisha Nutrition System — Full Test Suite
info.description:
Complete API test collection for the Maisha nutrition assistant. Covers 10 user profiles (6 original + 4 new), all edge cases, medication safety tiers, condition flags, WhatsApp intent classification, pantry management, and hydration engine.

Base URL: Set {{base_url}} = your Flask API root (e.g. http://localhost:5000)

Run order: Onboarding → Morning Plan → Edge Cases → Pantry → WhatsApp Webhook

item.name: ── USER 1 — Amina (Weight Loss, Sedentary, No Conditions) ──
01 Onboarding — Amina
item.description: EXPECT: BMR ~1420, TDEE ~1704, daily_kcal ~1204. System confirms 2-meal plan is valid for weight loss. No flags raised.

item.name: 02 Morning Plan — Amina (normal day)
item.description: EXPECT: Breakfast slot ~300 kcal / ~45-50 KES with category options (carb, protein, drink). Lunch slot ~700 kcal / ~100 KES. Water target 1.8L distributed across day. Eggs marked as pantry (zero cost). No dinner slot.

item.name: 03 Edge Case — Meal Skip + Time Constraint
item.description: EXPECT: intent=meal_skip+time_constraint. Lunch kcal target bumps from 700 to 900. Protein emphasis (eggs/beans). Hydration nudge (3 glasses now). No medication concern since Amina has none.

item.name: 04 Edge Case — Variety Request (tired of ugali)
item.description: EXPECT: intent=meal_feedback+variety_request. Ugali removed from rotation for 7 days. System returns 3 alternative carb combos (rice+beans, chapati+lentils, sweet potato+omena). Ugali scheduled to return next week.

item.name: 05 Edge Case — Progress Update (lost 1kg)
item.description: EXPECT: intent=progress_update. Milestone acknowledged. Projected goal timeline recalculated (~5 months at current rate). Next week's plan notes 50 kcal reduction as adaptation. Encouragement message.

item.name: 06 Onboarding — James (flags 2-large-meal pattern)
item.description: EXPECT: System flags 2-large-meal pattern. Recommends 5-meal split. Medication anchors set at 07:30 and 19:30. Ugali EXCLUDED (diabetes flag). Sugar EXCLUDED. White rice flagged (reduced portions). Carb ceiling 120g/day. 40g max per meal.

item.name: 07 Morning Plan — James (5-meal diabetic plan)
item.description: EXPECT: 5 slots: 07:30 (320 kcal ⚓), 10:30 snack (160 kcal), 13:00 lunch (480 kcal), 16:00 snack (160 kcal), 19:30 dinner (480 kcal ⚓). Metformin reminders at anchor slots. No ugali. No sugar. Water 2.2L.

item.name: 08 TIER 1 SAFETY — Breakfast Not Eaten at 8:15am
item.description: EXPECT: PRIORITY TIER 1 response. Metformin safety warning. Urgent instruction to eat something small NOW (biscuits/half banana). This must fire even if the user is inactive / hasn't engaged in days. Do NOT soften the urgency.

item.name: 09 Edge Case — Doctor Changes Carb Target
item.description: EXPECT: intent=goal_change+medical_instruction. Carb target updates from 120g to 80g. Sweet potato and brown rice removed. New carb sources listed (sorghum uji, millet, vegetables, legumes). System notes doctor instructions take priority.

item.name: 10 Edge Case — James Eats Ugali Anyway
item.description: EXPECT: intent=meal_log+condition_conflict. Gentle but clear flag that ugali is high-GI and will spike blood sugar. Suggest balance for dinner (low-carb, high protein). Remind of daily carb ceiling. No judgment — practical correction.

item.name: ── USER 3 — Brenda (H. Pylori, University Student, 80 KES) ──
11 Onboarding — Brenda (flags breakfast skip with medication)
item.description: EXPECT: Flag that 7am Amoxicillin requires food but breakfast is skipped. System presents 2 options (quick breakfast or schedule-based slot). Avoid: pilipili, coffee, acidic fruits. Budget split: breakfast 15 / lunch 35 / dinner 30 KES. Bulk pantry strategy suggested.

item.name: 12 Morning Plan — Brenda (ultra-budget 3-meal)
item.description: EXPECT: 3 medication-anchored slots. 07:00 quick breakfast (banana/biscuits, ~15 KES). 12:30 lunch (~35 KES, mama mboga options). 18:30 dinner (~30 KES, hostel options). Water 30 mins BEFORE meals not during. Coffee/soda/pilipili blocked.

item.name: 13 Edge Case — Ate Chips for Lunch
item.description: EXPECT: intent=meal_log+poor_nutrition. No shame. Practical correction: balance dinner with uji+eggs. H. Pylori note — uji is stomach-friendly. Remind to take 2pm Clarithromycin with something small if not yet taken.

item.name: 14 Edge Case — Treatment Complete
item.description: EXPECT: intent=medication_complete. H. Pylori anchors removed. Coffee/acidic foods cleared (slowly — 1 week). 3-meal pattern retained. Remind to retest in 4-6 weeks per doctor. Goal reverts to budget nutrition management.

item.name: 15 Edge Case — Budget Hit Zero Mid-Week
item.description: EXPECT: intent=budget_zero. System falls back to pantry-only suggestions. If pantry is also empty, acknowledges situation with dignity (no judgment). Suggests cheapest possible options (uji from bulk flour, plain ugali). Still reminds about medication timing.

item.name: ── USER 4 — Brian (Muscle Gain, Lactose Intolerant, Westlands) ──
16 Onboarding — Brian (protein/budget conflict detection)
item.description: EXPECT: BMR ~1820, TDEE ~3458, target 3758 kcal. Protein target 188g/day. System detects protein/budget conflict and proposes hybrid strategy (cheap proteins cover 120g, powder fills 70g). Lactose flag: no dairy milk/yoghurt — plant milk substituted. No dairy in any suggestion.

item.name: 17 Pre-Workout Plan — Brian (5:40am)
item.description: EXPECT: 5 meal slots. Pre-workout 300 kcal (oats+banana+coffee, pantry). Post-workout flagged PRIORITY with 30-min window. Total kcal ~3750. Protein est ~185g. Water 3.2L. No dairy in any slot. Plant milk noted.

item.name: 18 Edge Case — Rest Day (missed gym)
item.description: EXPECT: intent=activity_change. Rest day protocol. Calorie drops to ~2800. Pre-workout skipped. Post-workout replaced with light snack. All meals -20% portion. Muscle growth reminder (rest = growth).

item.name: 19 Edge Case — Protein Powder Depleted
item.description: EXPECT: intent=pantry_update+nutrition_gap. Powder marked depleted. 70g protein gap calculated. Budget reallocation: +2 eggs (+12g, +32 KES), omena to lunch (+20g, +30 KES), lentils to dinner (+18g, +25 KES). Gap partially covered. Restock advised.

item.name: 20 Edge Case — Dairy Accidentally Consumed
item.description: EXPECT: intent=allergy_exposure. Practical response — note what happened, suggest monitoring for symptoms (bloating, cramps). No panic. Rest of day plan avoids all dairy. Future suggestions reinforce dairy-free alternatives. No alarm if accidental small exposure.

item.name: ── USER 5 — Mary (Anaemia, Single Mother, Family Budget, Kibera) ──
21 Onboarding — Mary (family nutrition + iron medication timing)
item.description: EXPECT: Iron target 18mg/day. Tea-with-breakfast flag (reduces absorption — tea 1 hour after). Vitamin C pairing noted (+absorption). Family budget split: 200 KES / 4 people. Children protein priority. Mary personal iron priority. Cooking for 4 affects portion calculations.

item.name: 22 Morning Plan — Mary (family day, iron anchored)
item.description: EXPECT: Ferrous sulfate reminder at 06:00 (empty stomach). Breakfast at 06:30 (30 min later). Sorghum uji (iron-rich), eggs (protein for children), tomatoes (Vitamin C + iron absorption). Tea delayed to 07:30. Lunch for Mary alone (children at school). Dinner for family. Total ~170 KES.

item.name: 23 Edge Case — Children Refuse Beans
item.description: EXPECT: intent=family_feedback+meal_rejection. Three practical alternatives (coconut milk beans, mashed beans in ugali, githeri). Githeri suggestion with cost (~50 KES family). Iron and protein value preserved across alternatives.

item.name: 24 Edge Case — Mary Exhausted After Hard Day
item.description: EXPECT: intent=fatigue+emotional_state. Empathetic response. Suggest 15-min quick dinner (reheat yesterday's beans + ugali + raw tomato). No elaborate cooking required. Hydration note (fatigue often = dehydration). Compassion without being dismissive of her situation.

item.name: 25 Edge Case — Mary Skips Her Iron Tablet
item.description: EXPECT: intent=medication_skip. Iron tablet guidance: if stomach is not empty now, take with Vitamin C food (tomato/orange) to partially offset. Don't double dose. Reminder set for tomorrow 06:00. Note that consistent dosing is critical for anaemia treatment.

item.name: ── USER 6 — Kevin (H. Pylori + Ulcer, Erratic Schedule, Mombasa) ──
26 Onboarding — Kevin (multiple red flags, night owl schedule)
item.description: EXPECT: 3 red flags raised. Schedule built around 11am wake time (not standard morning). Medication anchors: Omeprazole 11:00 (empty stomach), then 11:30/15:00/22:00 with food. Calorie target 2200 (underweight surplus). Ulcer limits per-meal portion size. Street food flagged (re-used oil). Empty stomach danger noted.

item.name: 27 'Morning' Plan — Kevin (11:05am wake)
item.description: EXPECT: Omeprazole reminder first. 3 medication-anchored meal slots (11:30, 15:00, 22:00). Dinner kept light (ulcer + lying down). No coffee/pilipili/soda. Milk or uji recommended (coats stomach). Water 30 min before meals. ~100 KES total.

item.name: 28 TIER 1 SAFETY — Forgot Midday Medication + Food
item.description: EXPECT: PRIORITY TIER 1. Triple therapy without food + ulcer = high risk. Urgent instruction: eat something NOW (3-4 biscuits, bread, banana). Take medication immediately after. Confirm back when done. Do not soften or delay this response.

item.name: 29 Edge Case — Hungry at 2am
item.description: EXPECT: intent=hunger+unusual_time+ulcer_condition. Acknowledge 2am hunger. Empty stomach is dangerous for ulcer. Recommend: bread+milk (coats stomach), plain uji, or banana. Block spicy/fried/acidic. Note this is comfort eating not medication time.

item.name: 30 Edge Case — Side Effects from Triple Therapy
item.description: EXPECT: intent=side_effect+treatment_concern. Normalize triple therapy side effects (nausea, metallic taste, loose stomach). Practical help: always eat before dawa, probiotics/yoghurt, ginger tea. Severe symptoms (vomiting, rash, severe pain) = hospital. Complete the course — partial course creates resistance.

item.name: 31 Onboarding — Grace (hypertension, sodium restriction)

body.raw:
{
  "user_id": "USR007",
  "name": "Grace",
  "age": 48,
  "gender": "female",
  "weight_kg": 78,
  "height_cm": 162,
  "activity_level": "light",
  "primary_goal": "weight_loss",
  "budget_daily_kes": 180,
  "conditions": ["hypertension"],
  "allergies": [],
  "medications": [
    {
      "name": "Amlodipine",
      "dose": "5mg",
      "times": ["08:00"],
      "requires_food": false,
      "note": "can take with or without food, but consistency matters"
    }
  ],
  "meal_slots": ["breakfast", "lunch", "dinner"],
  "sodium_limit_mg": 1500,
  "pantry": [
    { "ingredient": "maize_flour", "tier": 1 },
    { "ingredient": "cooking_oil", "tier": 1 },
    { "ingredient": "salt", "tier": 1 }
  ],
  "location": "Eldoret"
}

item.description: EXPECT: Sodium limit 1500mg/day flagged across all meals. Salt in pantry marked as restricted (not zero, but flagged). Processed foods blocked (sausages, canned goods). Stock cube/royco flagged as high sodium. Potassium-rich foods prioritized (bananas, sweet potato, sukuma). DASH diet principles applied.

item.name: 32 Morning Plan — Grace (low-sodium day)
item.description: EXPECT: 3 meals within sodium budget. No processed meats. Royco/stock cubes absent. Potassium foods featured (bananas, sweet potato, sukuma, managu). Cooking instructions note: use herbs/lemon not salt for flavour. Amlodipine reminder at 8am (flexible with/without food).

item.name: 33 Edge Case — Used Royco in Cooking
item.description: EXPECT: intent=meal_log+sodium_flag. Gentle note that Royco is high sodium (~500mg per serving). Remaining sodium budget for the day reduced accordingly. Rest of day plan adjusted (very low sodium dinner). Not a crisis — practical recalibration.

item.name: 34 Edge Case — Blood Pressure Reading Shared
item.description: EXPECT: intent=health_metric_update. Acknowledge reading. 158/95 is elevated — note this, suggest logging to share with doctor. Reinforce today's low-sodium plan. Increase water intake recommendation. Suggest rest. IMPORTANT: system does NOT diagnose or prescribe — it flags and defers to doctor.

item.name: 35 Edge Case — Craving Salty Snack
item.description: EXPECT: intent=craving+condition_conflict. Acknowledge the craving (real, not weakness). Offer low-sodium alternatives: roasted groundnuts (unsalted), cucumber with lemon, plain popcorn. Explain salt craving connection to water intake — suggest large glass of water first.

item.name: ── USER 8 — Samuel (Kidney Disease, Fluid & Potassium Restricted) ──
36 Onboarding — Samuel (kidney disease, multiple restrictions)
item.description: EXPECT: MULTIPLE CRITICAL FLAGS. Fluid restricted to 1200ml (NOT the standard 35ml/kg formula). High-potassium foods BLOCKED (bananas, sweet potato, tomatoes, beans, managu). High-phosphorus blocked (dairy, nuts, legumes — major protein sources removed). System must flag that nutrition on CKD requires dietitian. Water target: 1200ml HARD CEILING. This is the hardest profile to serve.

item.name: 37 Morning Plan — Samuel (kidney-safe day)
item.description: EXPECT: Fluid tracked across ALL sources (food + water combined). Potassium-flagged foods absent. White rice (low potassium) featured. Egg whites over whole eggs (lower phosphorus). Furosemide anchor at 08:00 with food. System notes if full nutritional targets cannot be met safely — defers to dietitian.

item.name: 38 Edge Case — Ate Banana (high potassium)
item.description: EXPECT: intent=meal_log+condition_conflict (HIGH SEVERITY). One banana = ~422mg potassium. For a 2000mg daily limit this is 21% of budget in one item. Rest of day must be very low potassium. System adjusts remaining suggestions accordingly. Note: if this is a pattern, doctor must be informed.

item.name: 39 Edge Case — Drank Extra Water
item.description: EXPECT: FLUID SAFETY ALERT. 6 glasses ≈ 1500ml — over the 1200ml limit. This is a medical concern for CKD. System flags clearly: fluid limit exceeded today. No more water for the rest of the day. Recommends contacting doctor if swelling or breathlessness occurs. Direct, not alarmist.

item.name: 40 Onboarding — Rose (pregnancy nutrition, folic acid, iron)

body.raw:
{
  "user_id": "USR009",
  "name": "Rose",
  "age": 27,
  "gender": "female",
  "weight_kg": 67,
  "height_cm": 160,
  "activity_level": "light",
  "primary_goal": "nutrient_intake",
  "pregnancy_week": 18,
  "budget_daily_kes": 200,
  "conditions": ["pregnancy"],
  "allergies": [],
  "medications": [
    {
      "name": "Folic Acid",
      "times": ["08:00"],
      "requires_food": false
    },
    {
      "name": "Ferrous Sulfate",
      "times": ["10:00"],
      "requires_empty_stomach": false,
      "note": "take between meals for absorption"
    },
    {
      "name": "Calcium",
      "times": ["20:00"],
      "requires_food": false
    }
  ],
  "meal_slots": ["breakfast", "morning_snack", "lunch", "afternoon_snack", "dinner"],
  "pantry": [
    { "ingredient": "maize_flour", "tier": 1 },
    { "ingredient": "cooking_oil", "tier": 1 },
    { "ingredient": "rice", "tier": 1 }
  ],
  "location": "Meru"
}

item.description: EXPECT: Pregnancy calorie adjustment (+350 kcal 2nd trimester). Iron 27mg/day target (pregnancy doubles requirement). Folic acid 600mcg target. Calcium 1000mg/day. Foods to avoid: raw meat/fish, unpasteurized dairy, papaya (excess), pineapple (excess), alcohol. Folate-rich foods featured (dark leafy, lentils). Calcium-iron separation noted (take at different times). Morning sickness-friendly options available.

item.name: 41 Morning Plan — Rose (pregnancy 5-meal plan)
item.description: EXPECT: 5 smaller meals (nausea management). Folic acid reminder. Iron supplement between meals (not with tea/calcium). Folate-rich foods at multiple slots. Calcium at dinner slot. Raw egg/meat absent. Sorghum uji (iron), dark greens, lentils featured.

item.name: 42 Edge Case — Morning Sickness, Can't Eat
item.description: EXPECT: intent=nausea+pregnancy. Empathy first. Morning sickness management: dry crackers/plain biscuits before getting up. Ginger tea (safe in pregnancy, anti-nausea). Cold foods over hot (smell triggers nausea). Small amounts frequently. Hydration critical (vomiting = dehydration risk). If unable to keep anything down for 24h — see doctor (hyperemesis risk).

item.name: 43 Edge Case — Craving Unusual Food (ugali + soil/clay)
item.description: EXPECT: intent=pica_craving. This is PICA — a pregnancy phenomenon often signalling iron or zinc deficiency. DO NOT normalize eating soil/clay (it's dangerous — parasites, toxins). Explain what pica means (iron deficiency signal). Check iron supplement compliance. Suggest immediate ANC visit. Offer iron-rich food alternatives as craving redirection.

item.name: 44 Edge Case — Asks if Papaya is Safe
item.description: EXPECT: intent=food_safety_query+pregnancy. Nuanced answer: ripe papaya in small amounts is generally fine. Unripe/semi-ripe papaya contains latex which can cause contractions — AVOID. Papaya seeds also avoid. Suggest safer Vitamin C alternatives (orange, tomatoes). Always confirm with ANC provider.

item.name: 45 Onboarding — Joseph (elderly, diabetes + hypertension + arthritis)

body.raw:
{
  "user_id": "USR010",
  "name": "Joseph",
  "age": 68,
  "gender": "male",
  "weight_kg": 75,
  "height_cm": 170,
  "activity_level": "sedentary",
  "primary_goal": "diabetic_control",
  "budget_daily_kes": 300,
  "conditions": ["type2_diabetes", "hypertension", "arthritis"],
  "allergies": [],
  "medications": [
    {
      "name": "Metformin",
      "dose": "1000mg",
      "times": ["07:30", "19:30"],
      "requires_food": true
    },
    {
      "name": "Lisinopril",
      "dose": "10mg",
      "times": ["08:00"],
      "requires_food": false
    },
    {
      "name": "Diclofenac",
      "dose": "50mg",
      "times": ["12:00", "20:00"],
      "requires_food": true,
      "note": "NSAID — must take with food to protect stomach lining"
    }
  ],
  "meal_slots": ["breakfast", "mid_morning", "lunch", "afternoon", "dinner"],
  "dietary_texture": "soft_foods_preferred",
  "pantry": [
    { "ingredient": "maize_flour", "tier": 1 },
    { "ingredient": "rice", "tier": 1 },
    { "ingredient": "cooking_oil", "tier": 1 }
  ],
  "location": "Thika"
}

item.description: EXPECT: Complex multi-condition system. Diabetes: ugali excluded, sugar excluded, low-GI carbs, carb ceiling, 5-meal split. Hypertension: sodium restricted, potassium-rich foods. Arthritis: anti-inflammatory foods (turmeric, ginger, oily fish), soft textures preferred. Diclofenac: NSAID gastric risk — must ALWAYS have food. Metformin anchors. Lisinopril flexible. Conflicts: potassium-rich foods (good for BP) may conflict with CKD if any develops.

item.name: 46 Morning Plan — Joseph (multi-condition 5-meal)
item.description: EXPECT: 5 slots. Metformin anchors 07:30+19:30. Diclofenac at 12:00 and 20:00 — meals timed accordingly. Soft food options (well-cooked ugali alternatives, soft beans, boiled vegetables). No ugali, no sugar, low sodium. Anti-inflammatory additions (ginger tea, turmeric in stew). Water target with hypertension note (not excess).

item.name: 47 Edge Case — Joint Pain Flare, Can't Cook
item.description: EXPECT: intent=physical_limitation+pain. Empathy. No-cook options: bread + boiled egg (if pre-cooked), banana, plain uji (minimal effort). Medication critical: Diclofenac at 12:00 must still have food even if minimal. Remind that skipping Metformin food anchor is risky. Suggest asking family member to help today.

item.name: 48 Edge Case — Multiple Medications Conflict Query
item.description: EXPECT: intent=new_medication. Atorvastatin (statin) added to profile. Evening timing confirmed (statins work better at night). Food interaction: grapefruit juice blocks statin metabolism — flag and remove from suggestions. High-cholesterol foods now also flagged (fried foods, processed meats). System updates profile.

item.name: 49 Edge Case — Blood Sugar Very Low (hypoglycaemia signal)
item.description: EXPECT: PRIORITY TIER 1 — hypoglycaemia signal. Immediate instruction: eat fast-acting sugar NOW (half glass juice, 3 glucose sweets, or 1 tablespoon honey). Sit down. Do NOT take Metformin now. If dizziness persists or gets worse — call someone / go to hospital. System does not diagnose — it acts on the described symptoms and errs on the side of caution.

item.name: 50 Pantry — Declare Tier 1 Bulk Stock
item.description: EXPECT: Tier 1 items marked as bulk stock. Zero daily cost applied. Available freely in all suggestions. No budget charged.

item.name: 51 Pantry — Weekly Stock Update (eggs tray)
item.description: EXPECT: 30 eggs stocked. Cost amortized (~6 KES/egg). System tracks usage — decrements when eggs are selected. Prompts restock when estimated to fall below 5.

item.name: 52 Pantry — WhatsApp Text Depletion Report
item.description: EXPECT: intent=pantry_update. Eggs marked depleted. Removed from all suggestions immediately. Added to shopping list. Morning plan regenerated without eggs — alternative proteins suggested.

item.name: 53 Pantry — Shopping List Request
item.description: EXPECT: Consolidated shopping list based on depleted Tier 2/3 items + projected needs for next 3 days. Budget estimate included. Items sorted by priority (medication-critical items first).

item.name: 54 Hydration — Targets by Condition
item.description: EXPECT: Each user returns their specific target. USR004 (very active) = 3200ml. USR008 (CKD) = 1200ml hard ceiling (not formula-based). Condition adjustments applied. Targets clamped to 1500-4000ml range (except CKD which has a doctor-prescribed ceiling).

item.name: 55 Hydration — H. Pylori Water Timing Rule
item.description: EXPECT: H. Pylori-specific answer. Water DURING meals dilutes stomach acid needed for H. Pylori treatment. Drink 30 min BEFORE meals instead. This applies specifically to ulcer and H. Pylori conditions.

item.name: 56 Calorie Engine — BMR Calculations Audit
item.description: EXPECT: All 3 calculations return within ±50 kcal of expected values. Mifflin-St Jeor formula confirmed. Activity multipliers: sedentary=1.2, light=1.375, very_active=1.9. Deficit -500 for weight loss, surplus +300 for muscle gain, -200 for diabetic control.

item.name: 57 Condition Flag — Verify Exclusions
item.description: EXPECT: ugali=EXCLUDED, sugar=EXCLUDED, white_rice=REDUCED+FLAGGED, sweet_potato=ALLOWED (low GI), brown_rice=ALLOWED (small portion), sorghum_uji=PREFERRED, millet=PREFERRED, beans=ALLOWED.

item.name: 58 Condition Flag — H. Pylori Exclusions
item.description: EXPECT: coffee=BLOCKED, pilipili=BLOCKED, soda=BLOCKED, oranges=BLOCKED (acidic), ugali=ALLOWED, uji=PREFERRED (gentle), eggs=PREFERRED (boiled), milk=PREFERRED (coats stomach), ginger_tea=ALLOWED (anti-nausea).

item.name: 59 Intent Classification — Grok Multi-Class

body.raw:
{
  "messages": [
    { "id": 1, "text": "niko busy sana leo sina time ya breakfast",       "expected": "meal_skip+time_constraint" },
    { "id": 2, "text": "nimechoka na ugali kila siku",                     "expected": "meal_feedback+variety_request" },
    { "id": 3, "text": "nimepoteza kilo moja!",                             "expected": "progress_update" },
    { "id": 4, "text": "sijala breakfast bado",                             "expected": "medication_safety_tier1" },
    { "id": 5, "text": "doctor amesema nipunguze wanga zaidi",              "expected": "goal_change+medical_instruction" },
    { "id": 6, "text": "protein powder yameisha",                           "expected": "pantry_update+nutrition_gap" },
    { "id": 7, "text": "nimechoka sana leo kazi ilikuwa ngumu",             "expected": "fatigue+emotional_state" },
    { "id": 8, "text": "sijisikii vizuri dawa zinanisumbua",                "expected": "side_effect+treatment_concern" },
    { "id": 9, "text": "naona njaa sana",                                   "expected": "hunger+unusual_time" },
    { "id": 10,"text": "nimemaliza dawa yangu yote leo",                   "expected": "medication_complete" }
  ]
}

item.description: EXPECT: All 10 classifications correct. Note message #4 (sijala breakfast bado) must classify as medication_safety_tier1 WHEN the user has medication anchors — context-aware classification, not just text-based.

item.name: 60 Priority Tier — Medication Safety Always Fires
item.description: EXPECT: Even with inactive_user=true and 5 days no engagement, Tier 1 medication safety response STILL fires. This validates the priority tier system — safety overrides engagement-based message suppression. Response must be urgent, not batched.
